nextSession.thesis: PRE-MARKET (7/15, ~7:32 ET) off the 7/14 close, targeting today's regular session — the market is NOT closed. The cool June CPI and broad Q2 bank beats left the bull intact but the ex-dividend-adjusted cache reframes the index read: SPY 749.17 stays bull-stacked (+1.14% over its 50-day ~740.8, +8.33% over its 200-day ~691.6, −1.12% off the 52-week high), while QQQ 711.74 is TESTING its 50-day from just below (−0.54%, ~715.6) with a negative 5-day (mom5 −1.53) — the reclaim is NOT won. VIX 16.38 low/calm. Fresh 2-week RRG: XLE is the cleanest rotation INTO (accel +1.05, one RS-cross from Leading) as XLI rolls hardest (accel −2.40); XLF is now Peaking ⚠ (accel −0.17) — still the highest-RS sector (rsRatio 101.47) but its leadership is rolling, so HOLD financials rather than add; XLK/semis Weakening ↓ while stretched. Today's 8:30 ET June PPI + Morgan Stanley pre-open is the imminent catalyst. Posture into 7/15: HOLD the index bull-stack, ROTATE toward XLE over rolling XLI on its RS-cross, HOLD (do not chase) Peaking XLF, TRIM stretched semis into strength, and carry only a small defined-risk hedge through today's PPI. Same 7/14 daily close as the prior run — one observation of the structural thesis, not a new one.

actions.subject: SPY / QQQ / SPMO / VGT core longs
actions.rationale: The core is bull-stacked with strong medium-term momentum (SPY mom126/252 +9.23%/+21.04%; QQQ mom63 +16.6, mom252 +28.75; SPMO the #1 6M momentum rank at 27.44%). QQQ is TESTING its 50-day from just below (−0.54%, mom5 −1.53) — hold the structure, but this is a test-not-reclaim, so do NOT add index beta here.
actions.structuralAnchor: SPY 20>50>200; spot +1.14% over the 50-day (~740.8), +8.33% over the 200-day (~691.6), −1.12% off the 52-week high (~757.7). QQQ 711.74 bull-stacked but −0.54% BELOW its 50-day (~715.6). SPMO +20.48% / VGT +16.52% over their 200-days.
actions.trigger: Hold while SPY holds the rising 50-day (~741); add only on a QQQ RECLAIM of its 50-day (~715.6) with mom5 turning positive, NOT while it sits below with a negative 5-day.
actions.invalidation: A confirmed daily close below the SPY 50-day (~741), QQQ losing the 20>50>200 stack, or a broad loss of the QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacks.

actions.subject: Add XLE vs trim rolling XLI
actions.rationale: The fresh 2-week RRG keeps energy the cleanest rotation INTO: XLE Improving ↑ (accel +1.05, rsMom 101.48, rsMom6m +14.34, +11.25% over its 200-day) one cross from Leading, while XLI is the sharpest roll on the board (Weakening ↓, accel −2.40). The sector model's own read: 'Rotate toward XLE as XLI weakens.'
actions.structuralAnchor: XLE Improving quadrant, accel +1.05, rsRatio 99.65 (one cross from Leading), rsMom 101.48; XLI Weakening ↓, accel −2.40, rsRatio 100.26.
actions.trigger: Add XLE on its rsRatio crossing 100 into Leading; fund by trimming XLI on any failure of its rising 50-day.
actions.invalidation: XLE acceleration rolls back negative or oil (USO 29.6, Mixed, mom21 −12.29) breaks lower on a PPI-energy air-pocket, aborting the energy turn.

actions.subject: XLF (financials) — leadership now Peaking ⚠, hold don’t chase
actions.rationale: The Q2 bank beats made XLF the highest-RS sector (rsRatio 101.47), but on the fresh 2-week accel it has flipped to Peaking ⚠ (accel −0.17, rotation out) — leadership rolling at a high level. Per anti-whipsaw, HOLD existing financials and let Morgan Stanley (today, pre-open) inform the tape; do NOT add into the roll.
actions.structuralAnchor: XLF Peaking ⚠, rsRatio 101.47 (highest on the board), rsMom 100.61, accel −0.17, bull-stack (+7.35% over its 200-day).
actions.trigger: Re-add only if XLF re-accelerates (rsMom crossing back up / return to Leading) above its rising 50-day; a strong MS print is the potential catalyst.
actions.invalidation: XLF drops out of the top RS band (rsRatio back <100) or fails its rising 50-day — the bank-beat leadership fully fades and the hold becomes a trim.

actions.subject: Stretched semis (SOXX) into strength
actions.rationale: SOXX has the strongest medium-term structure on the board (+43.76% over its 200-day) but is Weakening ↓ on the 2-week accel (rsMom 99.09 <100, accel −0.34) and stretched far above trend. Trim the extension, keep the core — de-risking a stretched leader, not selling the AI-hardware theme.
actions.trigger: Trim on SOXX losing its rising 20-day / into relief strength; re-add only when rsMom crosses back above 100 with breadth improving.
actions.invalidation: SOXX reclaims a rising RS-momentum (>100) and turns rotation-into — the leadership re-accelerates and the trim was early.

actions.subject: Carry a defined-risk hedge through today's 8:30 PPI
actions.rationale: June PPI prints TODAY at 8:30 ET — the first pipeline-inflation confirm/deny to the cool CPI — with Morgan Stanley pre-open. VIX 16.38 is low and SPY sits near the highs, so carry only a small defined-risk put-spread / VIX-call stub into the number; reassess after PPI rather than paying for a standing short into the 7/29 FOMC.
actions.structuralAnchor: VIX 16.38 (low) with SPY −1.12% off the 52-week high — cheap vol plus today’s 8:30 PPI and an unverified two-way 7/29 policy risk create asymmetric event exposure.
actions.trigger: Carry a small defined-risk stub through today's PPI; reduce it if PPI confirms cooling and the tape holds, or roll toward 7/29 only after re-pulling the live FedWatch path.
actions.invalidation: A cooling PPI + benign MS narrows the near-term risk (let the stub decay), or VIX pushes >19-20 and the stub is already working.

dataAsOf.bars: Tier-A pre-market refresh generated 2026-07-15T11:32:15Z from 281 cached series, off the 7/14 regular-session close and targeting today’s (7/15) session. On the ex-dividend-adjusted cache: SPY 749.17 and QQQ 711.74 (bull-stacked, but QQQ −0.54% below its 50-day), SPMO 152.86, VGT 115.58, MSFT 390.99 (bear-stacked). The market is open today — this is the pre-open read, not a closed-market carry.
dataAsOf.options: The Tier-A snapshot surfaced 22 option chains in the pre-market slice. Re-pull the specific chain in the gamma/options-structure labs before sizing a level trade or the 7/17 OPEX pin; do not carry prior wall values as current.
dataAsOf.macro: VIX 16.38 (Yahoo ^VIX, pre-market refresh) is LOW/calm. Fear & Greed, VIX9D/VIX3M term structure and current FedWatch probabilities are not in Tier-A and must be re-pulled before sizing a term-structure or policy trade. WTI remains CARRIED/STALE.
dataAsOf.events: Near-term calendar, nearest-first: June PPI TODAY 7/15 8:30am ET + Morgan Stanley pre-open; June retail sales + weekly initial jobless claims 7/16 8:30am; monthly OPEX 7/17; FOMC 7/29 2:00pm ET and MSFT earnings AMC; July NFP 8/7 8:30am ET. Exact FOMC probabilities are STALE AND UNVERIFIED this run. Consensus figures remain ESTIMATES and must be re-verified at release.

vix.term: VIX 16.38 (Yahoo ^VIX, pre-market refresh) — LOW / calm and well under the 3-point regime-change threshold. Term structure (VIX9D/VIX3M) is not in Tier-A; re-pull it live before making a gamma-regime claim. Falsifier: VIX >19-20 or IVTS backwardation (>1.0).
vix.regimeLabel: calm — VIX 16.38; term structure not fetched

breadth.note: STRUCTURALLY BROAD but leadership is ROTATING, not cleanly Leading. Tool-aligned RRG (2-week RS accel, pre-market 7/15): XLE is the cleanest rotation INTO (Improving ↑, accel +1.05, rsMom 101.48, one cross from Leading, +11.25% >200d); XLI rolls hardest (Weakening ↓, accel −2.40). XLF is Peaking ⚠ (accel −0.17) — the highest RS on the board (rsRatio 101.47) but leadership rolling over. Tech/XLK Weakening ↓ (accel −0.14) yet still the top structural RS (+18.77% >200d); semis (SOXX) Weakening ↓ (accel −0.34, +43.76% >200d, extended). MAGS complex Improving ↑ (accel +1.48, rsMom 101.18) but only 1/7 bull-stacked. Proxy from sector-member bars, not an exchange breadth feed. Author rotation from RRG state/accel, NOT the raw 1-month RS level.

regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST, pre-market 7/15 off the 7/14 close: a healthy tech-led bull near all-time highs whose one near-term crack is NOT yet repaired. SPY 749.17 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −1.12% off its 52-week high, +8.33% over a rising 200-day, mom126/252 +9.23%/+21.04%. 4th distinct bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14) — the same 7/14 close as the prior run, one observation, not a new one. The cool June CPI + Q2 bank beats kept the bull intact, but on the ex-dividend-adjusted cache QQQ 711.74 is TESTING its 50-day from just below (−0.54%, mom5 −1.53) — the reclaim is NOT won. VIX 16.38 (low/calm). Three features: (1) leadership ROTATING not cleanly Leading — XLE the best accel (+1.05) one cross from Leading, XLI rolling hardest (−2.40), XLF Peaking ⚠ (highest RS but rolling), XLK/semis Weakening ↓ while stretched; (2) the lone STRUCTURAL crack MSFT (bear-stack, −27.41% off its high, −2.9% below its 50-day, mom5 +1.1 a minor tick) still has NOT reclaimed — the bearish relative tell into its 7/29 capex guide; (3) NO clean haven bid — GLD 14.9 / SLV 0 firmly Downtrend, BTC-USD ticked to Mixed (49.9) but IBIT still Downtrend (36); only agriculture (DBA 54.7) is a firm uptrend. Today's 8:30 PPI + Morgan Stanley is the imminent catalyst. Posture: HOLD the bull-stack; ROTATE XLE over rolling XLI; HOLD (don't chase) Peaking XLF; TRIM stretched semis into strength; carry a small defined-risk hedge through today's PPI; stay OUT of havens; do NOT call an MSFT bottom until it reclaims ~403.

backdrop.primaryTrend: Extended structural bull near all-time highs whose one near-term crack is NOT yet repaired (pre-market 7/15). SPY 749.17 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −1.12% off the 52-week high, +8.33% over a rising 200-day; SPMO/VGT bull-stacked; QQQ 711.74 bull-stacked but TESTING its 50-day from just below (−0.54%, mom5 −1.53) — reclaim not won. Leadership is ROTATING rather than cleanly Leading: XLE the best 2-week accel (one cross from Leading), XLI rolling hardest, XLF Peaking ⚠ (highest RS but rolling), XLK/semis Weakening ↓ while stretched. The single structural exception is MSFT (bear-stack, −27.41% off high) still below both MAs.
backdrop.trendEvidence: SPY mom126 +9.23 / mom252 +21.04, +1.14% over the 50-day (~740.8), +8.33% over the 200-day (~691.6). QQQ mom63 +16.6, mom252 +28.75, but −0.54% BELOW its 50-day (~715.6) with a negative 5-day (mom5 −1.53) — testing, not reclaimed. SPMO +20.48% / VGT +16.52% over their 200-days. Sector breadth broad but leadership rotating — XLE Improving ↑ (accel +1.05), XLI Weakening ↓ (accel −2.40), XLF Peaking ⚠. SOXX +43.76% over its 200-day (extended, Weakening ↓). Trend-intact readings; the cool CPI + bank beats kept the bull but did NOT win back the QQQ 50-day.

support: gamma put wall — re-pull live (Tier-A walls not surfaced)

resistance: 50-day ~715.6 (spot −0.54% BELOW — the reclaim line, not yet won)
QQQ.note: QQQ 711.74 is TESTING its 50-day from just below (−0.54%, mom5 −1.53) — the 20>50>200 stack still holds (bull-stack) but the reclaim is NOT won; a close back above ~715.6 with positive 5-day would repair it. MA levels from Tier-A ma-distance THIS run.

support: in a DOWNTREND — bear-stack; mom5 +1.1 (a minor tick, did NOT reclaim), no base confirmed
MSFT.note: MSFT 390.99 bear-stacked, −27.41% off its 52-week high (~538.7), and below both MAs — the LONE core name still broken. NOT a trend change until it reclaims the 50-day (~403). Idiosyncratic — QQQ/VGT/SPMO remain bull-stacked. The 7/29 capex guide is the tell.

backdrop.macroCycle: HAWKISH-HOLD Fed: funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings. The carried estimate for 7/29 was ~53.5% hold / ~46.5% hike, but that split is STALE AND UNVERIFIED this run (the CME fetch redirected without exposing probabilities). Treat 7/29 as a live two-way policy risk and re-pull FedWatch before sizing. Today’s 8:30 June PPI is the first forward inflation confirm/deny. WTI is carried/stale; USO is Mixed (score 29.6, mom21 −12.29).
globalBackdrop: GLOBAL ROTATION is Asia-led and confirms the onshore semis / AI-hardware supply chain: EWS (Singapore) leads at 73.7/100 vs ACWI (rel21 +9.05, Strong uptrend), EWT (Taiwan) 73 next (rel63 +24.2, rel126 +46.51), then EWC 59.2, EWY 56.5, EWJ 56.3, EWU 55.1 — but EVERY leader carries an FX headwind / confirms-weakness (EWS fxStrength −1.48, EWT −2.02, EWC −2.68, EWJ −2.0), so the equity strength is local-currency, not a weak-dollar tailwind (deep-link: global-rotation-lab.html).

AI-CAPEX CYCLE is the dominant secular driver — MSFT’s structural breakdown (bear-stack, −27.41% off its high, below both MAs) vs the AI-hardware/semis bull-stacks (VGT/SPMO near highs, SOXX +43.76% over its 200-day) IS the "show-me" (software) vs "build-it" (hardware/semis) dispersion at the single-name level. The MSFT capex guide 7/29 is the tell (deep-link: ai-capex-strategy-lab.html).

REAL ASSETS show NO clean haven bid: GLD firmly Downtrend (14.9, mom63 −16.01), SLV washed out (0, mom63 −24.49); BTC-USD ticked to Mixed (49.9, mom21 +5.9) but IBIT still Downtrend (36). Agriculture (DBA 54.7, Uptrend) is the only firm real-asset trend; broad commodities (DBC 42.7) and oil (USO 29.6) are Mixed. Net: risk-on / disinflationary-at-the-margin — leadership is equities, not havens (deep-link: real-assets-lab.html).
backdrop.pricedIn: Cool CPI and orderly bank beats were rewarded, but the QQQ 50-day was NOT won back. What remains uncertain is whether today’s 8:30 PPI confirms cooling, whether Morgan Stanley extends the bank tape, and whether MSFT’s failure to reclaim foreshadows a soft 7/29 capex guide. The exact Fed path is not verified this run.
backdrop.asymmetry: Downside remains more convex than upside over the next two weeks: SPY is only 1.12% below its 52-week high while VIX is 16.38. That cheap-vol/near-high combination supports a small defined-risk hedge through today’s PPI and, after re-verification, into the Fed; it does not justify adding a standing short.
backdrop.whatWouldChangeIt: A QQQ reclaim of its 50-day (~715.6) with positive 5-day (bullish repair), a confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~741, bearish), VIX pushing >19-20, term-structure inversion, a sticky 8:30 PPI today, or MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~403). The 7/29 FOMC is the next major policy regime-changer, but its current probabilities require a live re-pull.

attention.why: This is the near-term hold-or-fail line. HOLD the core; add index beta only on a RECLAIM of ~715.6 with mom5 turning positive, NOT while price sits below with a negative 5-day. A clean reclaim would repair the one near-term crack.

attention.structuralAnchor: MSFT 390.99 bear-stack, below both MAs (−2.9% / −11.17%), −27.41% off its high

attention.why: It’s the "show-me" (software) side of the AI-capex dispersion vs the "build-it" (semis) bull-stacks. NOT a trend change until it reclaims the 50-day (~403); the 7/29 FY26 Q4 capex guide (same day as the FOMC) is the binary for whether the software side re-rates.

events.consensus: ESTIMATE (re-verify at release) — 8:30 ET PPI (PCE feed-through); MS reports pre-open as the bank tape continues after the 7/14 beats

scenarios.name: confirms-cool
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bullish — PPI confirms the cool 7/14 CPI, PCE read-through benign, some of the ~46.5% hike tail eases

scenarios.name: inline
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: muted — PPI in line, the relief rally holds; MS extends or fades the bank-beat leadership

scenarios.name: confirms-hot
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bearish-at-margin — sticky pipeline PPI keeps the widened hike tail live despite the cool CPI (the Hormuz-energy path)

events.psychologyNote: The FIRST forward confirm/deny to the cool CPI. A confirming-cool PPI narrows the hike tail the relief tape is watching; a sticky PPI is the tell that the ~46.5% 7/29 hike is real. MS is the last of the big-bank tape — watch whether it extends the XLF Leading read.

events.event: June retail sales + weekly initial jobless claims
events.consensus: ESTIMATE (re-verify at release) — 8:30 ET; retail control group feeds the Q2 GDP nowcast; claims the labor read (XLY/XLP read-through)

scenarios.name: soft-landing
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: constructive — steady spend + contained claims = soft-landing intact, supports the confirmed XLF/XLE cyclical rotation

scenarios.name: weak
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: mixed — soft retail / rising claims stokes growth worry but eases the widened hike tail

scenarios.name: hot
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bearish-at-margin — strong spend + low claims compounds the ~46.5% hike tail into OPEX

events.psychologyNote: The growth-vs-inflation cross the day before OPEX: it tells you whether the consumer confirms the soft-landing the cyclical rotation (XLF Leading, XLE accelerating) is priced for — and whether hot data re-arms the 7/29 hike tail.

events.consensus: Standard monthly expiry — lands 3 days after the cool-CPI relief; with the binary resolved benign, positive dealer gamma should re-lengthen and pin the range unless PPI/retail surprise

scenarios.name: pin
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: neutral — positive-gamma pin dampens the range into expiry as the resolved-benign tape settles near the highs

scenarios.name: unwind-up
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bullish — a continued relief rally is amplified as hedges roll off toward the ATH (~757.6)

scenarios.name: unwind-down
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bearish — a hot PPI/retail surprise flips positive gamma and dealers sell into weakness

events.psychologyNote: The gamma release valve on the data week. With the CPI binary resolved benign and VIX at 16.5, a pin is plausible, but Tier-A has neither term structure nor wall levels; re-pull the chain live before assigning a dealer-gamma regime.

psychology.read: NAMED REGIME: a calm, positive-gamma, tech-led bull that RESOLVED its densest binary of the month benignly — the June CPI printed cool and the big banks beat broadly — but is NOT at fresh highs and has one unrepaired near-term crack. VIX 16.38, SPY bull-stacked but −1.12% off the ATH, and on the ex-dividend-adjusted cache QQQ 711.74 is TESTING its 50-day from just below (−0.54%, mom5 −1.53) rather than reclaiming it. The crowd's posture is RELIEVED-BUT-NOT-CONFIRMED — the fat left tail (hot CPI / bank miss) is gone, yet leadership is ROTATING (XLE accelerating in, XLI rolling out, XLF Peaking at a high level) rather than cleanly Leading. NOTE the deterministic composite score of 0 is VIX-only (Fear&Greed was NOT fetched this run), NOT a measured neutral and NOT a data gap — VIX is present and low (16.38). The subtle danger is COMPLACENCY: the crowd treats cool CPI as an all-clear on the Fed, but the 7/29 hike tail (carried ~46.5%, STALE/UNVERIFIED) is a live two-way risk and today's 8:30 PPI is the first forward confirm/deny. That is the underpriced near-term asymmetry. What would FALSIFY the read: a VIX push back >19-20 or term-structure inversion (regime flip), a confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~741) (bull failure), a sticky 8:30 PPI today, the market pricing/delivering the ~46.5% 7/29 hike (the biggest single falsifier), or — bullishly — QQQ reclaiming its 50-day (~715.6) with positive 5-day and MSFT reclaiming ~403 (a broadening that ends the lone crack).
inputs: SPY 749.17 bull-stack (20>50>200), +1.14% over the 50-day (~740.8), +8.33% over the 200-day (~691.6), −1.12% off the 52-week high (~757.7); mom126/252 +9.23% / +21.04% (Tier-A pre-market read, 7/15 11:32Z off the 7/14 close)

4th DISTINCT daily bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14); the SAME 7/14 close as the prior run — one observation, not a new one. QQQ 711.74 is TESTING its 50-day from just below (−0.54%, mom5 −1.53) — reclaim NOT won
June CPI WEB-VERIFIED cool (headline −0.4% MoM / core 0.0% MoM); Q2 big banks WEB-VERIFIED beat (GS, JPM, upbeat NII) — both carried from prior web research, not re-fetched this run

RRG rotation: XLE Improving ↑/into (accel +1.05, board best, one cross from Leading); XLI Weakening ↓/out (accel −2.40, sharpest roll); XLF Peaking ⚠ (rsRatio 101.47 highest but rolling); XLK/SOXX Weakening ↓; MAGS Improving ↑ (accel +1.48) on thin breadth (1/7 bull-stacked)
VIX 16.38 (low/calm, pre-market refresh); Fear&Greed NOT fetched → composite is VIX-only (score 0 = VIX-only, NOT a data gap). Term structure not in Tier-A (re-pull live)
Real assets: no clean haven bid — GLD 14.9 / SLV 0 firmly Downtrend; BTC-USD 49.9 Mixed but IBIT 36 Downtrend; DBA 54.7 the only firm uptrend (risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin)
Forward catalysts: TODAY 7/15 8:30 PPI+MS, 7/16 retail+claims, 7/17 OPEX, 7/29 FOMC+MSFT — the carried ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail is STALE/UNVERIFIED and must be re-pulled
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: The base case is a bullish slow-grind near the highs, but the leadership index has NOT reclaimed its 50-day and leadership is rotating, so the cleaner edge is PRESSING the confirmed rotation (rotate XLI→XLE, trim stretched semis) and HOLDING — not adding index beta at the highs or chasing Peaking XLF. The one probability tilt AGAINST the crowd: the ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail (STALE/UNVERIFIED) is underpriced by a relief tape treating cool CPI as an all-clear — carry that as the near-term asymmetry through today’s PPI (watch energy / gold as the tell), not a standing short. Re-fetch VIX + term structure and the live gamma walls before sizing anything off the vol/positioning read.

the lone structural crack (−27.41% off high, −11.17% below its 200-day); mom5 +1.1 a minor pre-market tick, NOT a 50-day reclaim

groups.note: MAGS is Improving ↑/into on the 2-week accel (+1.48) — the mega-cap complex turning UP even though only 1/7 members are bull-stacked (breadth 1/7). Leadership is narrow and top-heavy: AAPL sits AT its 52-week high (the only bull-stack) and META's +16.4 mom21 / +7.38 mom5 surge carry the complex, NVDA is firm (+4.08 mom5, tangled just under its 50-day), while MSFT (the crack, −27.41% off high, mom5 +1.1 a minor tick not a reclaim) and TSLA (bear-stack, mom5 −5.96) lag. An EARLY-accumulation signal under a weak-breadth surface — WATCH for broadening (>1/7 bull-stacked) and an MSFT 50-day reclaim (~403) to confirm. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

groups.label: Semiconductors

notable.reason: sharpest reversal (mom5 −12.71 / mom21 −13.86, −31.24% off its high) — the profit-taking tell under the surface, now below its 50-day
strongest trend (mom63 +118.08, +84.57% over its 200-day), bull-stacked but mom5 −3.2 fading with the group
most extended (mom63 +122.82, +100.15% over its 200-day) — the extension risk into any air-pocket, mom5 −4.85
the AI-hardware bellwether rolling on the 5-day (mom5 −6.69) right at its 50-day (−0.56%) — the internal-thinning tell

groups.note: SOXX is Weakening ↓/out on the 2-week accel (rsMom 99.09 <100) but keeps the board's strongest structure — 8/12 bull-stacked, 12/12 above the 200-day, +43.76% over it. The internals are THINNING under a strong ETF-level structure: above-50-day breadth fell to 4/12 and the 5-day is negative across the leaders (MRVL −12.71, TSM −6.69, LRCX −5.79, MU −4.85, KLAC −4.74, AMD −3.2), with MRVL (mom21 −13.86, −31.24% off its high) the profit-taking tell. AMD/MU/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC are all far above trend — the extension is the risk. TRIM the extension into any PPI-relief pop / on a lost 20-day; re-add when rsMom crosses back above 100 with above-50 breadth improving — de-risking a stretched leader with fading internals, not selling the AI-hardware theme. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

QQQ.status: $711.74 (7/13 adjusted bar) — TESTING its 50-day (~715.6), currently −0.54% BELOW it, after the cool CPI + bank beats could not reclaim the line into pre-market; still +11.70% ABOVE the 200-day (~637.2). This is the structural hold-or-fail line the prior runs flagged: a shallow pullback within the uptrend, not a breakdown, but the reclaim has NOT been won yet and the 2-week tech accel is soft (leader XLK Weakening ↓). HOLD; the defined-risk hedge stub can carry overnight through today's 8:30 PPI into the 7/29 FOMC — a decisive, persistent close below ~715.6 deepens the caution; a reclaim and hold above it re-arms the long.

SPMO.status: $152.86 (7/14 bar) — +2.35% over the 50-day (~149.4), +20.48% over the 200-day (~126.9); the strongest structural trend in the watchlist and the #1 6M balanced-momentum ETF (27.44%). Core long; HOLD, add on a clean higher-high — the momentum-factor core held the lead through the relief.

VGT.status: $115.58 (7/13 bar) — +0.18% over the 50-day (~115.4, only barely), +16.52% over the 200-day (~99.2); a top-3 risk-adjusted momentum ETF (signal 21.62, eased from 22.66). Pure-tech core long; HOLD, but the 50-day is the near line to watch given the tech accel cooling (XLK Weakening ↓) — it is riding its 50-day, not extended above it.

MSFT.status: $390.99 (7/13 bar) — BELOW both key MAs: −2.90% under the 50-day (~402.7) and −11.17% under the 200-day (~440.1). The lone structural crack that did NOT join the relief bounce while QQQ tested its 50-day and VGT/SPMO held their trends — a bearish relative tell. NOT a trend change either way until it reclaims the 50-day (~402.7); the 7/29 capex guide is the binary for the AI "show-me vs build-it" dispersion.

experimental.title: Pre-catalyst de-risk detector (intraday-fade + VIX-uptick + leader-flip) — binary-resolved post-mortem
experimental.note: Method: on the session BEFORE a Tier-1 macro binary, score a composite "de-risk tell" = sign(index close drift) + sign(VIX day-change) + count of sectors rolling out (Leading/Improving→Weakening/Peaking) on the fresh bar, then event-study the forward gap conditioned on it. POST-MORTEM this run: the pre-CPI setup fired a MODERATE de-risk tell (QQQ lost its 50-day, XLI Weakening ↓ accel −2.40 rolling out) — the crowd DID pre-hedge — and the binary then resolved BENIGNLY (CPI cool, banks beat), so QQQ gapped up toward the 50-day but has NOT yet reclaimed it (711.74 vs ~715.6, −0.54%). That is the "crowd already hedged → smaller adverse gap" branch of the hypothesis: a pre-print de-risk into a benign print produced a shallow relief, not a breakdown and not a clean breakout. PRE-MARKET 7/15: the SAME detector now watches TODAY's 8:30 ET PPI as the next binary — the de-risk tell into it is MILD (QQQ testing not losing the 50-day, VIX 16.38 subdued). One clean observation logged for the event-study; the VIX day-change SIGN leg still needs a persisted prior-day close (level present, delta not) to be fully computable. Inputs: open→close index drift, VIX day-change (persist prior close), per-sector RRG state transitions, the dated catalyst + its realized outcome. No owning tool captures the pre-catalyst de-risk state-transition timing today.

experimental.title: Rotation-flip detector (Leading→Peaking) — binary-resolved post-mortem
experimental.note: Method: flag any sector crossing out of a strong quad (Leading→Peaking / Improving→Weakening) with negative acceleration on a fresh bar, then event-study the forward 5/10-day sector return conditioned on whether the coincident catalyst confirmed or denied the roll. POST-MORTEM this run: on the fresh pre-market bar the detector flags TWO rolls — the XLI→XLE hand-off (XLI Weakening ↓ accel −2.40 rolling out while XLE Improves ↑ accel +1.05, rsMom 101.48, rotating in) AND a NEW XLF Leading→Peaking ⚠ flip after the money-center bank beats (a single-bar accel roll off a hot leadership run). The energy-over-industrials rotation is CONFIRMED and actionable; the XLF flip is one bar only, so per §6c anti-whipsaw its prior add was downgraded to hold/watch rather than acted on — the detector correctly separates a persistent cyclical hand-off from a not-yet-persistent leadership cooling. Inputs: per-sector rsRatio/rsMom/accel history + the sector's next scheduled catalyst + its realized outcome. No owning tool captures the state-transition timing today.
